NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. LORD, I cry out to You; Make haste to me! Give ear to my voice when I cry out to You.
NKJV.2: Let my prayer be set before You as incense, The lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.
NKJV.3: Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth; Keep watch over the door of my lips.
NKJV.4: Do not incline my heart to any evil thing, To practice wicked works With men who work iniquity;  And do not let me eat of their delicacies.
NKJV.5: Let the righteous strike me; It shall be a kindness. And let him rebuke me; It shall be as excellent oil; Let my head not refuse it. For still my prayer is against the deeds of the wicked.
NKJV.6: Their judges are overthrown by the sides of the cliff, And they hear my words, for they are sweet.
NKJV.7: Our bones are scattered at the mouth of the grave, As when one plows and breaks up the earth.
NKJV.8: But my eyes are upon You, O GOD the Lord; In You I take refuge; Do not leave my soul destitute.
NKJV.9: Keep me from the snares they have laid for me, And from the traps of the workers of iniquity.
NKJV.10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, While I escape safely.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. LORD<S>3068</S>, I cry<S>7121</S> unto thee: make haste<S>2363</S> unto me; give ear<S>238</S> unto my voice<S>6963</S>, when I cry<S>7121</S> unto thee.
KJV.2: Let my prayer<S>8605</S> be set forth<S>3559</S> before<S>6440</S> thee as incense<S>7004</S>; and the lifting up<S>4864</S> of my hands<S>3709</S> as the evening<S>6153</S> sacrifice<S>4503</S>. <sup>set: Heb. directed</sup>
KJV.3: Set<S>7896</S> a watch<S>8108</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, before my mouth<S>6310</S>; keep<S>5341</S> the door<S>1817</S> of my lips<S>8193</S>.
KJV.4: Incline<S>5186</S> not my heart<S>3820</S> to any evil<S>7451</S> thing<S>1697</S>, to practise<S>5953</S> wicked<S>7562</S> works<S>5949</S> with men<S>376</S> that work<S>6466</S> iniquity<S>205</S>: and let me not eat<S>3898</S> of their dainties<S>4516</S>.
KJV.5: Let the righteous<S>6662</S> smite<S>1986</S> me; it shall be a kindness<S>2617</S>: and let him reprove<S>3198</S> me; it shall be an excellent<S>7218</S> oil<S>8081</S>, which shall not break<S>5106</S> my head<S>7218</S>: for yet my prayer<S>8605</S> also shall be in their calamities<S>7451</S>. <sup>me; it shall be a: or, let the rigteous smite mee kindly, and reprove me; let not their precious oil break my head, etc</sup>
KJV.6: When their judges<S>8199</S> are overthrown<S>8058</S> in stony<S>5553</S> places<S>3027</S>, they shall hear<S>8085</S> my words<S>561</S>; for they are sweet<S>5276</S>.
KJV.7: Our bones<S>6106</S> are scattered<S>6340</S> at the grave's<S>7585</S> mouth<S>6310</S>, as when one cutteth<S>6398</S> and cleaveth<S>1234</S> wood upon the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.8: But mine eyes<S>5869</S> are unto thee, O GOD<S>3069</S> the Lord<S>136</S>: in thee is my trust<S>2620</S>; leave<S>6168</S> not my soul<S>5315</S> destitute<S>6168</S>. <sup>leave: Heb. make not my soul bare</sup>
KJV.9: Keep<S>8104</S> me from the snares<S>3027</S> <S>6341</S> which they have laid<S>3369</S> for me, and the gins<S>4170</S> of the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S>.
KJV.10: Let the wicked<S>7563</S> fall<S>5307</S> into their own nets<S>4364</S>, whilst that I withal<S>3162</S> escape<S>5674</S>. <sup>escape: Heb. pass over</sup>

ESV.1: O LORD, I call upon you; hasten to me! Give ear to my voice when I call to you!
ESV.2: Let my prayer be counted as incense before you, and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice!
ESV.3: Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth; keep watch over the door of my lips!
ESV.4: Do not let my heart incline to any evil, to busy myself with wicked deeds in company with men who work iniquity, and let me not eat of their delicacies!
ESV.5: Let a righteous man strike me — it is a kindness; let him rebuke me — it is oil for my head; let my head not refuse it. Yet my prayer is continually against their evil deeds.
ESV.6: When their judges are thrown over the cliff,  then they shall hear my words, for they are pleasant.
ESV.7: As when one plows and breaks up the earth, so shall our bones be scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
ESV.8: But my eyes are toward you, O GOD, my Lord; in you I seek refuge; leave me not defenseless!
ESV.9: Keep me from the trap that they have laid for me and from the snares of evildoers!
ESV.10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, while I pass by safely.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. O LORD, I call upon You; hasten to me! Give ear to my voice when I call to You!
NASB.2: May my prayer be counted as incense before You; The lifting up of my hands as the evening offering.
NASB.3: Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth; Keep watch over the door of my lips.
NASB.4: Do not incline my heart to any evil thing, To practice deeds of wickedness With men who do iniquity; And do not let me eat of their delicacies.
NASB.5: Let the righteous smite me in kindness and reprove me; It is oil upon the head; Do not let my head refuse it, For still my prayer is against their wicked deeds.
NASB.6: Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock, And they hear my words, for they are pleasant.
NASB.7: As when one plows and breaks open the earth, Our bones have been scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
NASB.8: For my eyes are toward You, O GOD, the Lord; In You I take refuge; do not leave me defenseless.
NASB.9: Keep me from the jaws of the trap which they have set for me, And from the snares of those who do iniquity.
NASB.10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, While I pass by safely.

NLT.1:
O LORD, I am calling to you. Please hurry!
Listen when I cry to you for help!
NLT.2:
Accept my prayer as incense offered to you,
and my upraised hands as an evening offering.
NLT.3:
Take control of what I say, O LORD,
and guard my lips.
NLT.4:
Don’t let me drift toward evil
or take part in acts of wickedness.
Don’t let me share in the delicacies
of those who do wrong.
NLT.5:
Let the godly strike me!
It will be a kindness!
If they correct me, it is soothing medicine.
Don’t let me refuse it.
But I pray constantly
against the wicked and their deeds.
NLT.6:
When their leaders are thrown down from a cliff,
the wicked will listen to my words and find them true.
NLT.7:
Like rocks brought up by a plow,
the bones of the wicked will lie scattered without burial.
NLT.8:
I look to you for help, O Sovereign LORD.
You are my refuge; don’t let them kill me.
NLT.9:
Keep me from the traps they have set for me,
from the snares of those who do wrong.
NLT.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets,
but let me escape.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. Yahweh, I have called on you. Come to me quickly! Listen to my voice when I call to you.
WEB.2: Let my prayer be set before you like incense; the lifting up of my hands like the evening sacrifice.
WEB.3: Set a watch, Yahweh, before my mouth. Keep the door of my lips.
WEB.4: Don’t incline my heart to any evil thing, to practice deeds of wickedness with men who work iniquity. Don’t let me eat of their delicacies.
WEB.5: Let the righteous strike me, it is kindness; let him reprove me, it is like oil on the head; don’t let my head refuse it; Yet my prayer is always against evil deeds.
WEB.6: Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock. They will hear my words, for they are well spoken.
WEB.7: “As when one plows and breaks up the earth, our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.”
WEB.8: For my eyes are on you, Yahweh, the Lord. In you, I take refuge. Don’t leave my soul destitute.
WEB.9: Keep me from the snare which they have laid for me, from the traps of the workers of iniquity.
WEB.10: Let the wicked fall together into their own nets, while I pass by.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David. [1] LORD, I call on you; hurry to help me.
Listen to my voice when I call on you. ⓙ
CSB17.2:
May my prayer be set before you as incense, ⓚ
the raising of my hands as the evening offering. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
LORD, set up a guard for my mouth;
keep watch at the door of my lips. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
Do not let my heart turn to any evil thing
or perform wicked acts
with men who commit sin.
Do not let me feast on their delicacies. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
Let the righteous one strike me —
it is an act of faithful love;
let him rebuke me —
it is oil for my head;
let me [1] not refuse it. ⓐ
Even now my prayer is against
the evil acts of the wicked. [2] ⓑ
CSB17.6:
When their rulers [3] will be thrown off
the sides of a cliff,
the people [4] will listen to my words,
for they are pleasing. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
As when one plows and breaks up the soil,
turning up rocks,
so our [5] bones have been scattered
at the mouth of Sheol. ⓓ
CSB17.8:
But my eyes look to you, LORD, my Lord.
I seek refuge in you; do not let me die. [6] ⓔ
CSB17.9:
Protect me from the trap they have set for me,
and from the snares of evildoers. ⓕ
CSB17.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets,
while I pass by safely. ⓖ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. LORD, I call upon You; hurry to me.
Listen to my voice when I call to You.
AMP.2:
Let my prayer be counted as incense before You;
The lifting up of my hands as the evening offering.
AMP.3:
Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth;
Keep watch over the door of my lips [to keep me from speaking thoughtlessly].
AMP.4:
Do not incline my heart to [consent to or tolerate] any evil thing,
Or to practice deeds of wickedness
With men who plan and do evil;
And let me not eat of their delicacies (be tempted by their gain).
AMP.5:
Let the righteous [thoughtfully] strike (correct) me—it is a kindness [done to encourage my spiritual maturity].
It is [the choicest anointing] oil on the head;
Let my head not refuse [to accept and acknowledge and learn from] it;
For still my prayer is against their wicked deeds.
AMP.6:
Their [wicked, godless] judges are thrown down the sides of the rocky cliff,
And they [who followed them] will hear my words, for they are pleasant (just).
AMP.7:
As when the one plows and breaks open the ground [and the soil scatters behind him],
Our bones have been scattered at the mouth of Sheol [by the injustices of the wicked].
AMP.8:
For my eyes are toward You, O GOD, the Lord;
In You I take refuge; do not pour out my life nor leave me defenseless.
AMP.9:
Keep me from the jaws of the trap which they have set for me,
And from the snares of those who do evil.
AMP.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets,
While I pass by and safely escape [from danger].

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, come close. Come quickly!
Open your ears—it's my voice you're hearing!
MSG.2:
Treat my prayer as sweet incense rising;
my raised hands are my evening prayers.
MSG.3:
Post a guard at my mouth, God,
set a watch at the door of my lips.
MSG.4:
Don't let me so much as dream of evil
or thoughtlessly fall into bad company.
And these people who only do wrong—
don't let them lure me with their sweet talk!
MSG.5:
May the Just One set me straight,
may the Kind One correct me,
Don't let sin anoint my head.
I'm praying hard against their evil ways!
MSG.6:
Oh, let their leaders be pushed off a high rock cliff;
make them face the music.
MSG.7:
Like a rock pulverized by a maul,
let their bones be scattered at the gates of hell.
MSG.8:
But God, dear Lord,
I only have eyes for you.
Since I've run for dear life to you,
take good care of me.
MSG.9:
Protect me from their evil scheming,
from all their demonic subterfuge.
MSG.10:
Let the wicked fall flat on their faces,
while I walk off without a scratch.

NIV.1:
Psalm 141
A psalm of David.
O Lord, I call to you; come quickly to me.
Hear my voice when I call to you.
NIV.2:
May my prayer be set before you like incense;
may the lifting up of my hands be like the evening sacrifice.
NIV.3:
Set a guard over my mouth, O Lord;
keep watch over the door of my lips.
NIV.4:
Let not my heart be drawn to what is evil,
to take part in wicked deeds
with men who are evildoers;
let me not eat of their delicacies.
NIV.5:
Let a righteous man strike me—it is a kindness;
let him rebuke me—it is oil on my head.
My head will not refuse it.
Yet my prayer is ever against the deeds of evildoers;
NIV.6:
their rulers will be thrown down from the cliffs,
and the wicked will learn that my words were well spoken.
NIV.7:
They will say, “As one plows and breaks up the earth,
so our bones have been scattered at the mouth of the grave.”
NIV.8:
But my eyes are fixed on you, O Sovereign Lord;
in you I take refuge—do not give me over to death.
NIV.9:
Keep me from the snares they have laid for me,
from the traps set by evildoers.
NIV.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets,
while I pass by in safety.

NRSVCE.1:
I call upon you, O LORD; come quickly to me;
give ear to my voice when I call to you.
NRSVCE.2:
Let my prayer be counted as incense before you,
and the lifting up of my hands as an evening sacrifice.
NRSVCE.3:
Set a guard over my mouth, O LORD;
keep watch over the door of my lips.
NRSVCE.4:
Do not turn my heart to any evil,
to busy myself with wicked deeds
in company with those who work iniquity;
do not let me eat of their delicacies.
NRSVCE.5:
Let the righteous strike me;
let the faithful correct me.
Never let the oil of the wicked anoint my head,
for my prayer is continually against their wicked deeds.
NRSVCE.6:
When they are given over to those who shall condemn them,
then they shall learn that my words were pleasant.
NRSVCE.7:
Like a rock that one breaks apart and shatters on the land,
so shall their bones be strewn at the mouth of Sheol.
NRSVCE.8:
But my eyes are turned toward you, O GOD, my Lord;
in you I seek refuge; do not leave me defenseless.
NRSVCE.9:
Keep me from the trap that they have laid for me,
and from the snares of evildoers.
NRSVCE.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets,
while I alone escape.

NET.1: O LORD, I cry out to you. Come quickly to me! Pay attention to me when I cry out to you!
NET.2: May you accept my prayer like incense, my uplifted hands like the evening offering!
NET.3: O LORD, place a guard on my mouth! Protect the opening of my lips!
NET.4: Do not let me have evil desires, or participate in sinful activities with men who behave wickedly. I will not eat their delicacies.
NET.5: May the godly strike me in love and correct me! May my head not refuse choice oil! Indeed, my prayer is a witness against their evil deeds.
NET.6: They will be thrown down the side of a cliff by their judges. They will listen to my words, for they are pleasant.
NET.7: As when one plows and breaks up the soil, so our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
NET.8: Surely I am looking to you, O sovereign LORD. In you I take shelter. Do not expose me to danger!
NET.9: Protect me from the snare they have laid for me, and the traps the evildoers have set.
NET.10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, while I escape.
